content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. For each kkk (1≤k≤N1 \le k \le N1≤k≤N), let's define a function f(k)f(k)f(k) in the following way:


Consider a sequence B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, which is created by setting Ak=0A_k = 0Ak​=0. Formally, Bk=0B_k = 0Bk​=0 and Bi=AiB_i = A_iBi​=Ai​ for each valid i≠ki \neq ki=k.
f(k)f(k)f(k) is the number of ways to split the sequence BBB into two non-empty contiguous subsequences with equal sums.
Find the sum S=f(1)+f(2)+…+f(N)S = f(1) + f(2) + \ldots + f(N)S=f(1)+f(2)+…+f(N).
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains an integer N on the next line, followed by N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the sum S.

solution.approach: For each test case, we will iterate over each element in the array and calculate the prefix sum. We will store the prefix sum in a hash map. Then, we will iterate over the array again and for each element, we will check if the difference between the current prefix sum and the prefix sum at the previous position exists in the hash map. If it does, we will increment the count of ways to split the array. Finally, we will return the sum of all counts.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * log(S)) where S is the maximum prefix sum

code.Python3:
T = int(input())
while T > 0:
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    s = [0] * (n + 1)
    cnt = [0] * (10 ** 9 + 1)
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        s[i] = s[i - 1] + a[i - 1]
        cnt[s[i]] += 1
    sum = 0
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        sum += cnt[s[i] - s[i - 1]]
    print(sum)
    T -= 1
